issues.comment: Empty array check `multiEmailValue.length === 0` will never be true because multiEmailValue is initialized with `[""]` (length 1), causing the function to always proceed even with only empty strings, which may cause validation errors later.

Email logic is inverted: `if (newGuests.includes(attendee.email))` sends AttendeeScheduledEmail to new guests but AttendeeAddGuestsEmail to existing attendees, when it should be the opposite - existing attendees should get add guests notification, new guests should get scheduled notification.

Permission check uses `isTeamAdmin(user.id, booking.eventType?.teamId ?? 0) && isTeamOwner(user.id, booking.eventType?.teamId ?? 0)` requiring user to be BOTH admin AND owner, but should typically use OR (||) to allow either role, making the permission too restrictive.

Blacklisted email filtering calls `email.toLowerCase()` on blacklisted emails but compares against guest emails that may not be lowercased, potentially missing matches and allowing blacklisted guests to be added.

Database update at line 92-106 succeeds before calendar update at line 164, but calendar update has no error handling - if calendar update fails, guests are added to database but not calendar, leaving inconsistent state.
Parameter reassignment `value = value || []` modifies the input parameter which can cause unexpected behavior and makes the function impure, should use a local variable instead.
Array splice operation `updatedValue.splice(index, 1)` lacks bounds checking - if index is somehow invalid, it could cause unexpected array modification or errors.
